title: passthrough fuse mounts to host?
body:
Is it possible? So I could see the same mounts on host I mounted inside the container.
Also filed for Toolbox: https://github.com/containers/toolbox/issues/429

comments.body: We need a lot more information about what you are asking?

Assuming that this is for Rootless - unfortunately, I don't think this is possible, as it's a kernel limitation. The user namespace that rootless Podman runs inside creates what is effectively a separate mount table, which the kernel does not allow to mix with the table on the host.

Hm, what should be the solution then?
I have an app ultimate goal of which is to create that mount - it's a distributed FS: https://book.keybase.io/docs/files
And it doesn't need root to operate. It works perfectly fine within Toolbox.
Of course, it's highly advisable to have that mount point accessible on the host also.

It's undesirable to install it on host because I run immutable OS - Fedora Silverblue.
Can you run a rootful podman and do your commands, I think this is supported on Silverblue.
~~/proc/PID/root might be what you want~~ (**EDIT**: this doesn't work across userns boundaries)
@AkihiroSuda could you explain what do you mean please?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@bam80 Are you still interested in this?
@rhatdan yes, probably, just don't have time on it now. I'm not sure Toolbox provides rootfull containers variant..

> I'm not sure Toolbox provides rootfull containers variant..

You are right about this one. It's coming in the future.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since this is currently impossible with the kernel, I am going to close.  If new kernel features come about, we could reopen and consider.
